discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with SPY +1%, IWM +2.5%, SMH +3.8%, XBI +3.9%, and oil/energy down sharply on Iran de-escalation signals. Movers cluster in semis (LSCC, ONTO, PLAB, QRVO) and airlines (ALK, SKYW) — both groups beneficiaries of lower oil + risk-on rotation. Most 8-Ks today are 5.07 (annual meeting vote results), which are non-catalytic; the real driver is macro tape, not company-specific news.

notable_index_moves: SMH +3.81% leading; semis broadly bid alongside small-caps (IWM +2.5%)
XLE -2.43% on Trump 'final stages' Iran talks and oil below $100
Airlines bid on lower fuel cost expectations

discoveries.name: Alaska Air Group
discoveries.setup: Airline sympathy spike on oil collapse + Iran de-escalation headlines
discoveries.thesis: ALK's outsized move appears driven entirely by the Iran-talks/oil-down narrative rather than any company-specific catalyst — the only 8-K is a 5.07 annual meeting vote, which is non-event. Move magnitude exceeds SKYW (peer) and the broader IWM by a wide margin on volume only 1.2x average, suggesting short-cover/momentum chasing into a single macro headline that can reverse if Iran talks stall. Fuel tailwind is real but lower-quality balance sheet airlines often overshoot peers on these reflex bids.
discoveries.what_confirms: Oil bounces back on Iran talks stalling; ALK gives back outsized portion relative to SKYW/peers; no positive company-specific news emerges in next 48h
discoveries.what_kills: Confirmed Iran framework agreement with sustained oil decline; ALK-specific positive guidance or route news; sector-wide upgrade cycle from sell-side
discoveries.what_to_learn: Single-headline oil-down airline rallies tend to overshoot in lower-quality names vs. peers — fade candidates when the move is 2-3x peer magnitude on average volume
discoveries.catalyst: Oil below $100 on Trump Iran 'final stages' comments; fuel-cost relief narrative
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Only 8-K is 5.07 (vote results, non-material); move size vs. SKYW peer (10% vs 6%) on similar tape implies sympathy/momentum, not company news
research_pointers: Compare ALK fuel hedge book vs SKYW — is leverage to oil really 2x?
Check ALK short interest; reflex rallies in heavily-shorted airlines decay fast
Watch oil tape Wednesday/Thursday for Iran follow-through

discoveries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap semi spike with no detectable catalyst on a strong SMH day
discoveries.thesis: LSCC up sharply on 1.4x volume with empty catalyst_signals. SMH +3.8% explains some of it but LSCC tripled that. Possible drivers: sell-side note, M&A whisper, or AI-FPGA narrative tailwind — but I cannot identify which from available evidence. Neither bull nor bear case clears conf 3.
discoveries.what_confirms: n/a — need new evidence (analyst note, leaked deal chatter, or company release) to form a directional view
discoveries.catalyst: Unidentified; SMH +3.8% tape provides partial explanation but not full magnitude
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {} — no 8-K, no earnings nearby
research_pointers: Check for sell-side initiations/upgrades on LSCC today
Scan for M&A chatter — Lattice has been a perennial takeout candidate
Compare LSCC vs other FPGA/programmable logic names for sector-specific lift

discoveries.name: Builders FirstSource
discoveries.setup: Housing-bill tailwind + 8-K with management change on risk-on tape
discoveries.thesis: House approved housing affordability bill today (removed industry-opposed build-to-rent requirements — explicitly framed as 'win for investors'). BLDR is a direct beneficiary as the largest building products supplier. Move is on only 1.02x volume despite a meaningful policy catalyst, and the stock is still negative on a 5-day basis — suggesting the housing-bill news hasn't been fully absorbed by funds yet. The 8-K's 5.02 officer item adds some noise but appears coincident with annual meeting (5.07 alongside), likely board-rotation routine.
discoveries.what_confirms: Senate signals on housing bill passage; XHB/ITB peers continue to bid; BLDR holds gains tomorrow on rising volume
discoveries.what_kills: 5.02 item turns out to be unplanned CFO/CEO departure; Senate kills bill; housing data prints weak
discoveries.what_to_learn: Policy catalysts that are explicitly framed as 'investor wins' in major media often underdiffuse on day-one because they require legislative-tracking specialists to map company exposure
discoveries.catalyst: House passes housing affordability bill (CNBC: 'win for investors'); 8-K with 5.02 director/officer changes around annual meeting
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K combines 5.02 + 5.07 + 7.01 on same date as annual meeting, suggesting routine board action; primary catalyst is the housing-bill headline that maps directly to BLDR's business
research_pointers: Read 5.02 exhibit to confirm director rotation vs surprise departure
Check XHB, LEN, DHI, BLD reaction magnitudes vs BLDR for sector confirmation
Map BLDR revenue exposure to build-to-rent vs traditional single-family

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation
discoveries.setup: Semicap name rebounding into SMH +3.8% tape after 5-day weakness
discoveries.thesis: ONTO was down 5% over the prior five sessions and is now recovering on a strong semi-equipment tape. The 8-K items (5.07 vote results, 8.01 other) are routine. Magnitude is in line with semicap peers given SMH +3.8%. No mispricing thesis stronger than 'mean reversion + sector beta' is articulable.
discoveries.catalyst: SMH +3.81% sector lift + mean reversion from prior week weakness
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Both 8-Ks contain only 5.07/8.01/9.01 items — no material company-specific news; move is sector-driven
research_pointers: Track ONTO vs KLAC/AMAT/LRCX relative strength for sector positioning

discoveries.name: Deckers Outdoor
discoveries.setup: Pre-earnings positioning into after-hours print tomorrow
discoveries.thesis: DECK reports tomorrow after the close. The move is consistent with risk-on consumer cyclical positioning (XLY +2.5%) into a high-beta earnings name. No mispricing — this is a coin flip on the print, and the magnitude is well within normal pre-earnings drift for DECK.
discoveries.what_confirms: n/a — observational; thesis is 'wait for the print'
discoveries.catalyst: Earnings tomorrow after close + consumer discretionary rally
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: upcoming_earnings shows 1 day to print, after-hours timing
research_pointers: Check DECK options skew/IV into print
HOKA brand momentum data points (search/foot traffic) before close tomorrow

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's biggest movers (LSCC +11.7%, ALK +10%, PLAB +8.9%, VCYT +8.4%, BLDR +7.3%, ONTO +6%, SKYW +6.2%, ALGN +5.3%, QRVO +4.5%, DECK +4.2%, KTOS +4.4%) do not have clean AI-news linkages to today's announcements. LSCC/ONTO/PLAB/QRVO moves look like a semiconductor sector bid (possibly sympathy to broader AI capex narrative or sector rotation) rather than a reaction to any specific item in the news set. ALK/SKYW are airlines — unrelated to AI. VCYT/ALGN/DECK/BLDR/KTOS have idiosyncratic catalysts (earnings, 8-Ks, annual meetings). No high-confidence AI-driven irrational selloff or insufficient rally identified in this dataset. Quiet night for the specific 'AI announcement triggers stock move' thesis Michael is hunting.
